refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the latency metric and end-to-end scope clarifies the performance requirement and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under representative network conditions and verify it meets ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over Network Link.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting for safety compliance.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce communication hops.
DP3: Optimize telemetry data encoding and compression for minimal payload.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive retransmission strategies.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependencies between Vehicle and Teleoperation
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates latency control via network protocol design, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of unexpected network congestion causing latency spikes.
Assumes Vehicle and Teleoperation modules support required protocol enhancements.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP2 (edge computing) if network conditions degrade or latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for remote control and communication latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting communication with latency guarantees.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains multiple functions beyond teleoperation latency control.
Contains telemetry endpoints but limited direct control over latency protocols.
Primary module responsible for latency management and protocol implementation.
Focuses on safety alerts, not latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control DP is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize protocol management and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from Vehicle-side telemetry implementation not fully controlled by Teleoperation.
Network Link variability outside module ownership.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate with Vehicle module to ensure telemetry endpoints support latency protocols; consider cross-module interface agreements.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies refined FR with clear latency control approach and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk due to Vehicle telemetry endpoints impacting latency.
Network Link variability outside Teleoperation control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and is appropriate ownership boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via Telemetry and Network Link interfaces; ownership clarity supports verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation IM with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface agreements with Vehicle module for telemetry latency support.
Monitor network conditions and consider fallback DP (edge computing) if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, risks related to network variability and Vehicle telemetry support require refinement and interface strengthening before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over Network Link.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting for safety compliance.

revise.why: Refine interface definitions and ownership boundaries to mitigate diffusion risks and clarify telemetry latency responsibilities.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle module supports required telemetry protocol enhancements.
open_risks: Insufficient telemetry support on Vehicle side.
evidence_needed: Latency performance measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
